findings: Verified identity: American Crow (Corvus brachyrhynchos), family Corvidae.
Measurements printed on the plate exactly match Audubon: 16–21 in (41–53 cm), wingspan 34–39 in (86–99 cm), weight 11–22 oz (311–623 g).

The illustration matches the supplied reference photos and verified field marks: all-black glossy plumage with subtle blue-violet sheen, robust black bill, dark brown-black eye, black/slate legs and strong feet, broad wing, and short square-to-slightly-rounded tail rather than a raven-like wedge.
Behavior, habitat, social-family, communal-roost, ground-foraging, and methodical-flight claims are supported by the live Cornell and Audubon pages.
No material factual, text, species, or anatomical errors found. The plate shows one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail; no extra or missing parts are visible.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: American Crow Overview — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
